message:
fix(core): correct three-valued NULL logic for NOT, BitNot, and Plus unary ops

evaluate_having_value():
- Added proper handling for UnaryOp::BitNot: returns ~integer for integer
  input, NULL for NULL input (previously fell through to catch-all returning
  NULL for all unary ops, which was only accidentally correct for BitNot)
- Added proper handling for UnaryOp::Plus: passthrough (identity operation)

eval_join_expr():
- Fixed NOT NULL to return NULL per SQL three-valued logic instead of
  incorrectly returning Integer(1). In SQL, NOT NULL = NULL, not TRUE.
- Fixed ~NULL (bitwise NOT of NULL) to return NULL instead of falling
  through to an incorrect code path

New regression tests in fsqlite/src/lib.rs:
- not_null_returns_null: verifies SELECT NOT NULL returns NULL
- not_null_in_where_excludes_row: verifies WHERE NOT flag correctly
  excludes rows where flag is NULL
- bitnot_null_returns_null: verifies SELECT ~NULL returns NULL

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
